stories: Good morning Sean, it's Saturday, the second of May, 2026
A nice one to start your weekend: a typical fluffy cumulus cloud weighs around a million tonnes, roughly the same as a hundred and fifty Eiffel Towers
The reason it stays up there is that the warm, moist air carrying it is just slightly less dense than the air around it
So next time one drifts past your window, picture a few thousand elephants quietly riding on a thermal
Your calendar is clear today, so the long weekend rolls on uninterrupted
Closer to home, a couple of Singapore stories worth knowing
And a lighter one: as part of Singapore HeritageFest 2026, organisers are launching a first-of-its-kind theatrical show staged on board a bumboat, celebrating the city's maritime history
A nice excuse to wander down to the river
Good evening Sean, it's Friday evening, the first of May, 2026
Here's a quietly lovely one to start your evening: when Apollo 16 astronaut Charles Duke walked on the moon back in 1972, he left behind a small family photo of himself, his wife, and his two boys
It's still up there, fifty-four years later, sitting in the lunar dust where no wind will ever move it
Your calendar is clear for tomorrow, so Saturday looks fully open
On home turf, Prime Minister Lawrence Wong delivered his May Day Rally today, and the headline line was a sharp one
As AI reshapes whole industries, he said, the government cannot promise to protect every job, but it will commit to protecting every worker
The labour movement also pledged thirty-seven million dollars this year to help workers and their families adapt
Worth listening to in full when you have a moment
